INTRO-01-a.mp3: Hey, it's Justin. Welcome to this meditation. I appreciate you taking the time to spend with me and your inner world. Make sure you've got a comfy enough position and environment.
INTRO-01-b.mp3: Hi, it's Justin. Welcome back to another self-regulation practice. Before you start, make sure your environment and position work well enough for you.
PINTRO-ANCHORING-a.mp3: This one is an anchoring practice. The whole point is simple — we're going to find safety in the present moment and settle into it. Nothing to fix, nowhere to get to. Just anchoring.
PINTRO-ANCHORING-b.mp3: In this practice, we're simply going to anchor into safety. We'll use your senses to connect with the present moment and let your system settle. That's the whole thing.
PINTRO-IMAGERY-a.mp3: This is an imagery and invitation practice. We'll anchor into safety first, then gently bring a non-safety emotion into view — give it an image, and invite it to be here with us. We're not fixing it. We're getting familiar with it.
PINTRO-IMAGERY-b.mp3: Up next is imagery and invitation. We anchor in safety, then we'll picture a non-safety experience and invite it to stay with us as we reconnect. Just getting to know it.
PINTRO-OBSTACLES-a.mp3: This is an obstacles practice. We'll anchor into safety, then I'll offer a few statements that tend to bump into our defenses. Whatever surfaces, we'll validate it, normalize it, and invite it to be with us.
PINTRO-OBSTACLES-b.mp3: In this practice, we work with obstacles. After we anchor in safety, I'll read a few statements, and we'll notice what pushes back inside you. Then we validate, normalize, and invite that in.
PINTRO-BALANCING-a.mp3: This is a balancing practice. We'll anchor into safety, then invite a little non-safety alongside it — and practice holding both at once. Safety stays the priority the whole way through.
PINTRO-BALANCING-b.mp3: Up next is balancing. We anchor in safety, bring in some non-safety, and let the two be present together. We're building the capacity to hold both, with safety leading.
PINTRO-PENDULATING-a.mp3: This is a pendulating practice. We'll anchor in safety, then gently move back and forth — a little non-safety, back to safety, and again — building flexibility between the two. Safety is always home base.
PINTRO-PENDULATING-b.mp3: In this one, we pendulate. After anchoring, we'll swing our attention between safety and non-safety, back and forth, letting your system learn it can move between them. We always come back to safety.
MIND-01-a.mp3: On a scale of 0 to 10, what is your current level of safety?
MIND-01-b.mp3: On a scale of 0 to 10, what is your current level of safety?
MIND-02-a.mp3: How is your body breathing? Check in with the natural ins and outs of your breath. How is your body naturally breathing right now? Don't change your breathing, just notice it. If it changes by itself because you've paid attention to it, that's fine. Chest or belly? Full or shallow? Tense or relaxed?
MIND-02-b.mp3: How is your body breathing? Check in with the natural ins and outs of your breath. How is your body naturally breathing right now? Don't change your breathing, just notice it. If it changes by itself because you've paid attention to it, that's fine. Chest or belly? Full or shallow? Tense or relaxed?
MIND-03-a.mp3: What do you hear? Anything? Silence? Inside the room… what do you hear? Outside the room… what do you hear? What sounds are catching your attention? Which ones come and go? Which ones stay?
MIND-04-a.mp3: How is your body breathing? Not controlling. Chest or belly? Full or shallow? Tense or relaxed?
MIND-04-b.mp3: How is your body breathing? Of course, not controlling it. Chest or belly? Full or shallow? Tense or relaxed?
MIND-05-a.mp3: What do you touch? We'll start with your entire body. What is it you're sitting, standing, lying, or leaning on? Can you feel its firmness? Or is it soft? Does it let you sink into it? What can you feel specifically on your skin? The air? The fabric of your clothes? Something you can hold onto nearby?
MIND-06-a.mp3: What do you see? Open your eyes if they're closed, as they are ready to open. You don't need to do anything special — just look where they want you to look. If your neck wants to move to help your eyes see more, let your neck and eyes work together. Is there one thing that is pulling at your eyes? What's its shape? Let your eyes trace the edges. Notice how the light from the room touches it. And the shadows it creates.
MIND-06-b.mp3: What do you see? Open your eyes if they're closed, as they are ready to open. You don't need to do anything special — just look where they want you to look. If your neck wants to move to help your eyes see more, let your neck and eyes work together. Is there one thing that is pulling at your eyes? What's its shape? Let your eyes trace the edges. Notice how the light from the room touches it. And the shadows it creates.
MIND-07-a.mp3: Is there an emotion you're holding onto?
MIND-07-b.mp3: Is there an emotion you're holding onto?
MIND-08-a.mp3: Invite it to be with you. If so, just let it be. Acknowledge it and invite it. Just noticing for now.
MIND-08-b.mp3: Invite it to be with you. If there is, invite it to be with you as you continue. We don't reject it.
MIND-08-c.mp3: Invite it to be with you. If so, just let it be. Acknowledge it and invite it. Just noticing for now.
MIND-08-d.mp3: Invite it to be with you. If there is, invite it to be with you as you continue. We don't reject it.
MIND-09-CAPCHECK-a.mp3: Go ahead and check your capacity now.
MIND-09-CAPCHECK-b.mp3: Do a quick capacity check now. Listen to your ability, willingness, openness, and curiosity to continue.
LADDER-TOUCH-01-a.mp3: Identify one thing in your space.
LADDER-TOUCH-01-b.mp3: Pick one thing in your space you'd like to focus on a little more.
LADDER-TOUCH-02-a.mp3: If there's an emotion you're carrying around, now's a good time to acknowledge it. Say hi to it. Let it be, but also give yourself permission to focus on my voice.
LADDER-TOUCH-02-b.mp3: You might have an emotion you're carrying around. You probably do. We all do. We don't ignore or reject it. Instead, acknowledge it and let it hang out while you also pay attention to my voice.
LADDER-TOUCH-02-c.mp3: If there's an emotion you're carrying around, now's a good time to acknowledge it. Say hi to it. Let it be, but also give yourself permission to focus on my voice.
LADDER-TOUCH-02-d.mp3: You might have an emotion you're carrying around. You probably do. We all do. We don't ignore or reject it. Instead, acknowledge it and let it hang out while you also pay attention to my voice.
LADDER-TOUCH-03-b.mp3: Bring your attention to that one thing you picked.
LADDER-TOUCH-04-a.mp3: Does it give you a sense of like or dislike? What about it do you like?
LADDER-TOUCH-04-b.mp3: First, does it give you a sense of like or dislike? What about this thing draws you to it?
LADDER-TOUCH-05-a.mp3: What does it feel like on your skin? Does it feel different on different parts of your hand? Fingertips? Palm? Knuckles? Wrist?
LADDER-TOUCH-05-b.mp3: What does it feel like on your skin? Check your fingertips, your palm, your knuckles, your wrist. And stick with whichever one feels the best and let your hand and this thing interact in whatever way they want to.
TAIL-01-BREATH-a.mp3: As you mindfully experience it, how does it affect your breath? Are you breathing into your chest or belly? Full or shallow? Tense or relaxed? Not controlling. Just noticing and being. Your body knows how to breathe and you're just paying attention.
TAIL-01-BREATH-b.mp3: As you mindfully experience it, how does your body breathe? I want you to pay attention to one breath at a time. On the next one — into your chest or belly? The next — full or shallow? The next — tense or relaxed?
TAIL-02-POSTURE-a.mp3: As you mindfully experience it, how does it affect your posture? Upright or collapsed? Energized or relaxed?
TAIL-02-POSTURE-b.mp3: As you mindfully experience it, how does it affect your posture? Upright or slumped? Energized or calm?
TAIL-03-SMILE-a.mp3: As you mindfully experience it, are you more or less likely to smile? Let yourself smile if so.
TAIL-03-SMILE-b.mp3: As you mindfully experience it, are you more or less likely to smile? Go ahead and smile if so. I'll smile with you.
TAIL-04-CONNECT-a.mp3: Do you feel more or less connected…? …to the present? To your senses? Your breath? Your inner experience?
TAIL-04-CONNECT-b.mp3: Do you feel more or less connected…? …to the present? To your senses? Your breath? Your inner experience?
SAFETY-01-NAME-a.mp3: What emotion word best fits your current safety experience? Calm? Still? Playful? Joy? Curious? Something like…
SAFETY-01-NAME-b.mp3: Let's give your current level of safety a name. What emotion word best fits it? Something like open or motivated or curious or playful? Maybe just still? And it's okay to say, I feel ____ but not very much.
SAFETY-02-GENERAL-a.mp3: Notice how safety feels in your body generally. Mobile or immobile? Heavy or light? What's the general head to toe experience of it?
SAFETY-02-GENERAL-b.mp3: How does this safety emotion feel overall? What's the general experience of it? More mobile or immobile? Heavy or light?
SAFETY-03-SPECIFIC-a.mp3: And what does this emotion feel like, more specifically? Is there one place in your body where it's even more obvious? Where it's coming from or going to? Check your face, chest, and belly if you're struggling.
SAFETY-03-SPECIFIC-b.mp3: Notice how safety feels in your body specifically. Is there one place in your body where this emotion is even more obvious? A place where it feels like it's coming from, or going to?
LADDER-SIGHT-01-a.mp3: Let your eyes gaze however they like. Not controlling them. Let them be and do what they want. If they want to gaze, let them gaze. If they want to dance around, let them dance around.
LADDER-SIGHT-01-b.mp3: Let's pay attention to what you see. Just let them be for a moment.
LADDER-SIGHT-02-a.mp3: Notice what your eyes are drawn towards. What's grabbing their attention in this moment? A color? A movement? A texture? How about now? What's grabbing your eyes' attention in this moment?
LADDER-SIGHT-02-b.mp3: And bring your attention to whatever they're showing you. You're not controlling. Just noticing. What's pulling at your eye? What's saying, hey, look over here?
LADDER-SIGHT-03-a.mp3: How do your eyes interact with the world around you? Do they want to focus? Gaze? Browse? Let them, don't get in the way of your eyes and the external world.
LADDER-SIGHT-03-b.mp3: How do your eyes want to interact with this thing? Let them take your attention wherever and however they want. It might change moment to moment.
LADDER-SOUND-01-a.mp3: Let's pay attention to what you hear. Notice the sounds outside your space. Beyond the room — what reaches you from further away?
LADDER-SOUND-01-b.mp3: Now pay attention to what you hear. What sounds do you hear outside of your immediate space? The ones further away?
LADDER-SOUND-02-a.mp3: Now bring your hearing inside your space. Right around you — what's here, close by?
LADDER-SOUND-02-b.mp3: Bring your hearing awareness to your more immediate space. What sounds do you hear here? Anything?
LADDER-SOUND-03-a.mp3: Notice what comes and goes. What sounds pop into existence and then leave?
LADDER-SOUND-03-b.mp3: What sounds come and go? Are here and then gone?
LADDER-SOUND-04-a.mp3: What sounds stay constant? Which ones stay and linger?
LADDER-SOUND-04-b.mp3: Any sounds that are kinda always there, lingering? The ones that are under the other sounds that come and go?
LADDER-SOUND-05-a.mp3: Notice the silence too. The spaces between sounds — the quiet they arrive into and leave behind.
LADDER-SOUND-05-b.mp3: Can you notice and connect with the silence underneath it all? The stillness of the present moment?
LADDER-SOUND-06-a.mp3: Let each sound be temporary. Nothing to hold onto. One moment, then the next — sound is always passing through.
LADDER-SOUND-06-b.mp3: Each sound will remain or come and go as it pleases. One moment after the next. All you need to do is pay attention.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-01-a.mp3: Up next, we're going to focus on movement. Gentle and small and easy. Let your body guide you on size and speed. But I recommend starting small, simple, and stable.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-01-b.mp3: We're going to move a little coming up, gently, on purpose. Nothing big. Ultimately, listen to your body's needs regarding size and speed and ability. But we'll start small and slow.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-02-a.mp3: Start with rolling your ankles. Slow, both directions. No strain. Do a windshield wiper move if you prefer.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-02-b.mp3: Let's roll the ankles. Slow circles, both ways. Nothing that strains.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-03-a.mp3: Now your wrists. Let your hands soften if that feels right. Fists are fine, too.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-03-b.mp3: Now the wrists. Easy circles, and let your hands soften as you go if that feels right.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-04-a.mp3: How about rolling your shoulders. Up, back, and down — let them drop.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-04-b.mp3: Roll the shoulders. Up, back, and down. And let them drop.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-05-a.mp3: Roll your neck. Small arcs and then get bigger as it loosens or feels right.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-05-b.mp3: Now, slowly roll your neck. Just small arcs and then widen if your neck wants to.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-06-a.mp3: Now, let yourself stretch whatever wants to lengthen. A reach upward, or a slight side bend. Just elongate something, really. If it feels better to go back to your ankles or wrist, go right ahead.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-06-b.mp3: Stretch and lengthen wherever your body wants to. Small and easy. Just pick something and go with it. If something else wants to stretch, too, then let it.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-07-a.mp3: Nicely done. Now, choose one movement to return to. Whichever felt best, or most available — spend more time with that.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-07-b.mp3: Pick one movement to come back to. The one that felt the best, or was the easiest. Let's spend a little more time with that as it's comfy. If you prefer stillness now, that's fine, too.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-08-a.mp3: Focusing on that one movement, intentionally make it bigger, smaller, or still.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-08-b.mp3: With that one movement going, play with it. Make it bigger, make it smaller, or bring it to stillness. Notice what feels best and stick with it.
LADDER-MOVEMENT-09-a.mp3: What does the movement feel like in your body? Where do you feel it most? In the joint, the muscle, the whole limb? How does the rest of your body respond?
LADDER-MOVEMENT-09-b.mp3: And what does that movement actually feel like? Where do you feel it the most? Is it the joint? The muscle? The whole limb? What does it feel like in the parts directly connected to the movement?
LADDER-IMAGINATION-01-a.mp3: Bring to mind a place where you feel at ease. Real or imagined — a memory, or made up. Somewhere you'd like to be.
LADDER-IMAGINATION-01-b.mp3: Picture a place where you feel… peace. It can be real or made up. A memory, or somewhere you'd just like to be.
LADDER-IMAGINATION-02-a.mp3: In this scenario, let your eyes be as they want.
LADDER-IMAGINATION-02-b.mp3: And let your eyes do whatever they want — open, closed, somewhere in between.
LADDER-IMAGINATION-03-a.mp3: What do you see in your imagination? Indoors or out? Day or night? What's in front of you? What are you doing?
LADDER-IMAGINATION-03-b.mp3: Notice what you see in your imagination.
LADDER-IMAGINATION-04-a.mp3: Look around the scene. What colors? What light sources? What's moving? What are you on top of?
LADDER-IMAGINATION-04-b.mp3: What colors do you see in your imaginative scene? What textures? What's moving? What's above you? Below you?
LADDER-IMAGINATION-05-a.mp3: What do you hear there? Or is it quiet?
LADDER-IMAGINATION-05-b.mp3: And what do you hear in this place? Or is it quiet here?
LADDER-IMAGINATION-06-a.mp3: What do you feel there? The air, the temperature, the ground beneath you?
LADDER-IMAGINATION-06-b.mp3: What can you feel? The air, the temperature, the ground underneath you?
LADDER-IMAGINATION-07-a.mp3: What's one thing in this space that brings you sense of peace? Or calm? Pay a little more focused attention to it.
LADDER-IMAGINATION-07-b.mp3: Is there something in this scene that's pulling at your attention? Something calling to you?
LADDER-IMAGINATION-08-a.mp3: Can you touch? If so, please do. Can you see or hear it? If so, please do.
LADDER-IMAGINATION-08-b.mp3: What about this thing do you like?
CURIOSITY-01-a.mp3: Do you feel interest or curiosity about your non-safety internal experiences? You can say yes or no.
CURIOSITY-01-b.mp3: Are you curious about your non-safety experiences right now? Any interest in turning toward them? It's okay to say yes or no.
CURIOSITY-01-c.mp3: Do you feel interest or curiosity about your non-safety internal experiences? You can say yes or no.
CURIOSITY-01-d.mp3: Are you curious about your non-safety experiences right now? Any interest in turning toward them? It's okay to say yes or no.
INTENT-SET-a.mp3: We're going to be very intentional about connecting with non-safety. So, I want you to choose an emotion to invite and pay attention to now. Give it a name. And we'll pay more attention to it later on. It can be a present moment emotion. Or the recent past? Something coming up within you? Something lingering for a long, long time? And stay open to Obstacles as they surface, but start with one.
INTENT-SET-b.mp3: Take a moment now and pick a non-safety emotion to work with. Could be right now, the recent past, something ahead of you, or something that's just been hanging around. Stay open to anything that might come up, too. But you're going to choose an emotion now.
INVITE-01-a.mp3: What is the non-safety emotion you'd like to invite? I feel _____.
INVITE-01-b.mp3: Is there a non-safety emotion you'd like to invite in? Fill in the blank — I feel _____.
INVITE-01-c.mp3: What is the non-safety emotion you'd like to invite? I feel _____.
INVITE-02-a.mp3: Does it make sense that you have this non-safety emotion, based on your life context?
INVITE-02-b.mp3: Given everything in your life, does it make sense that you'd feel this? It probably does.
INVITE-03-a.mp3: Is it okay for this non-safety emotion to be? You can say yes or no.
INVITE-03-b.mp3: Can you let this non-safety emotion just be here? You can say yes or no.
INVITE-04-a.mp3: On a 0 to 10 scale, what's the intensity level of this non-safety emotion?
INVITE-04-b.mp3: Give it a number. On a scale of 0 to 10, how intense is this non-safety emotion?
INVITE-05-a.mp3: How does this non-safety emotion feel in your body generally? Mobile or immobile? Heavy or light?
INVITE-05-b.mp3: How does it feel in your body, generally? More mobile or immobile? More heavy or light?
INVITE-06-a.mp3: How does this non-safety emotion feel in your body specifically? Is there one place where it's even more obvious? Coming from, or going to? Common areas: face, chest, and belly.
INVITE-06-b.mp3: And more specifically? Is there one spot where it's strongest? A place it seems to come from, or go to? Check your face, chest, and belly.
PEND-SAFETY-a.mp3: Bring your attention back to the safety experience.
PEND-SAFETY-b.mp3: And come back to safety. Back to that safety experience.
PEND-SAFETY-Q-b.mp3: Notice — has safety changed at all?
PEND-DEFENSE-a.mp3: Now bring your attention back to the non-safety experience.
PEND-DEFENSE-b.mp3: And back to the non-safety experience.
PEND-DEFENSE-Q-b.mp3: Notice — has the non-safety experience changed?
PEND-SAFE-REMIND-a.mp3: Remind yourself that you're literally safe.
PEND-SAFE-REMIND-b.mp3: And remind yourself — you are literally safe, right now.
PEND-TO-DEFENSE-BRIEF-a.mp3: And back to the non-safety experience.
HOLD-WATCH-a.mp3: Now hold the two at the same time if you can. Stay open to whatever wants to shift, if it does. Do they want to interact… or not?
HOLD-WATCH-b.mp3: Hold both at once. Remain open to potential shifts — do they want to interact, or not? Either is fine. You're just watching with curiosity and compassion.
HOLD-WATCH-c.mp3: Now hold the two at the same time if you can. Stay open to whatever wants to shift, if it does. Do they want to interact… or not?
IMG-01-a.mp3: What's one non-safety experience you can Validate? Say, I do feel _____. I don't like it, but I feel _____. Yeah, fine, I feel _____.
IMG-01-b.mp3: Name one non-safety experience and Validate it. I do feel _____. Or, I don't like it, but I feel _____. Or even, Yeah, fine — I feel _____.
IMG-02-a.mp3: Now Normalize that experience. I feel _____, which makes sense based on my life context.
IMG-02-b.mp3: Normalize it. I feel _____, and that makes sense, given my life context.
IMG-03-a.mp3: What does it look like? _____ looks like _____.
IMG-03-b.mp3: If it had a look, what would it be? _____ looks like _____.
IMG-04-a.mp3: Where is it? In your physical space, where is it? External or internal? Near or far?
IMG-04-b.mp3: And where is it — within you, or around you somewhere?
IMG-05-b.mp3: Is it doing anything? Moving, or still?
IMG-06-a.mp3: Invite it to be with you while you reconnect with the external world.
IMG-06-b.mp3: Now invite it to stay with you as you come back to the world around you.
OBST-01-a.mp3: I'm going to read four statements. Just notice what surfaces as you hear each one. Which of these four do you have a reaction to and what is it?
OBST-01-b.mp3: Four statements coming up. There's nothing to do but notice what comes up as you hear them. Notice which statement you have a stronger but tolerable reaction to and hang onto it if you can.
OBST-02-a.mp3: Validate the Obstacle. Give it a name.
OBST-02-b.mp3: Go ahead and Validate the Obstacle with an emotion word.
OBST-03-a.mp3: Normalize the Obstacle based on your life context. Does it make sense you feel this way in reaction to that statement?
OBST-03-b.mp3: And Normalize it. If someone else had your life would they have a similar reaction to that statement?
OBST-04-a.mp3: Now give it an image. What does it look like? Where is it? What is it doing?
OBST-04-b.mp3: Picture this emotion. What does it look like, where is it, and what's it doing?
OBST-05-a.mp3: Invite the Obstacle to be with you while you reconnect with the external world.
OBST-05-b.mp3: Invite the Obstacle to stay with you as you come back to the outside world.
OBSTACLE-STATEMENTS-a.mp3: I'm capable of achieving my goals.
OBSTACLE-STATEMENTS-c.mp3: My feelings are a normal result of my life context.
OBSTACLE-STATEMENTS-d.mp3: I am worthy of making change.
OBSTACLE-STATEMENTS-f.mp3: I have everything I need within me.
OBSTACLE-STATEMENTS-h.mp3: I am capable of creating positive change.
OBSTACLE-STATEMENTS-i.mp3: I am enough as I am.
OBSTACLE-STATEMENTS-j.mp3: I'm allowed to take up space.
OBSTACLE-STATEMENTS-m.mp3: It's okay to lower my defenses.
OBSTACLE-STATEMENTS-p.mp3: Things can get better for me.
CLOSE-01-a.mp3: Let your awareness begin to widen. No rush. You're going from the internal world back to the external, so let it come to you as you're ready to receive it.
CLOSE-01-b.mp3: Let your awareness start to open back up to the outside world. There's no rush. Let one little piece in at a time.
CLOSE-02-a.mp3: Use your ears and your eyes. If your eyes are closed, let them open when they're ready. What do you see? What do you hear?
CLOSE-02-b.mp3: Your ears and eyes will help you reconnect. If your eyes are closed, let them open when they're ready. What do you see? What do you hear?
CLOSE-03-a.mp3: Let your body move however feels good — or stay just as you are. Stretch, shift, settle. Maybe start with a stretch and see where it takes you.
CLOSE-03-b.mp3: Let your body move however it wants — or stay right where you are, whatever feels good. No right way or wrong way. Stretch, shift, settle. If you've been moving, feel free to keep going.
CLOSE-04-b.mp3: And take one full, intentional breath.
CLOSE-05-a.mp3: Offer yourself something kind — no fake affirmations. It has to be real. A simple recognition for showing up is good enough.
CLOSE-05-b.mp3: Give yourself a genuine kind word or two if you have it. No performative affirmations or gratitude. It has to be real.
OUTRO-a.mp3: That's it for this one. Really nicely done — showing up and doing this work is the hard part, and you did it. Take care of yourself now: move a little, get some water, get some air. I'll see you in the next one.
OUTRO-b.mp3: And that's our practice. Thank you for spending this time with yourself. Be good to your system on the way out — water, movement, rest, whatever it needs. I'll see you next time.
ENDING-OPEN-a.mp3: I'll let you continue on your own from this point. There's no need to come back yet. Stay here as long as you like, and end whenever you're ready. Listen to your internal world and let it take you where it needs.
ENDING-OPEN-b.mp3: From here, you can keep going on your own. No need to rush back. Stay as long as feels right, and end whenever you're ready.
LOOP-GATE-a.mp3: We've come back to safety. From here, you can keep going, or bring it to a close — either one is good. If there's more you want to stay with, just do nothing, and in a few seconds we'll go around again, starting right here from safety. If you feel complete, go ahead and tap end, and I'll walk you gently back.
LOOP-GATE-b.mp3: And we're back in safety. Notice — is there more you'd like to be with, or does this feel like a good place to finish? To keep going, just stay with me; we'll begin again from here in a moment. To stop, tap end, and we'll close gently together. No wrong choice.
SLOW-MOVE-a.mp3: And remember — nothing has to happen here. You don't have to make anything move or shift; just being with it is the whole practice. But if you notice an impulse to move — a stir, a need to shake, stretch, or push — you can let it. Just go slowly. Slow enough that you stay anchored in safety while that energy moves through you. The slowness is what keeps you connected.
SLOW-MOVE-b.mp3: There's no agenda. Nothing needs to change for this to be working. And if an impulse to move shows up — to squeeze, stretch, tremble, push, pull — let your body have it, slowly. Slow enough that you stay with safety as the energy releases. Let it move through at its own pace.
STOP-OK-a.mp3: And if this is too much, you can stop right here. Just say, I'm done — and that's a win. You anchored into safety, and that matters. You can always come back to this when you're ready.
STOP-OK-b.mp3: If at any point it's too much, you have full permission to stop. Anchoring in safety is already a success. Come back to the rest another time. No pressure at all.
NUMB-OK-a.mp3: And if you're not feeling much — or anything at all — that's okay. Numbness is a real experience too. You're not doing this wrong. Just notice the not-feeling, with as little judgment as you can.
NUMB-OK-b.mp3: If there's nothing there, or it's hard to feel, that's completely okay. The blankness or numbness is information, not failure. Just be with the not-much-ness for a moment.
CAP-GATE-a.mp3: Before we go further — this next part moves toward harder feelings, the non-safety stuff. Take a second and check: do you have the capacity, and the genuine willingness, to go there right now? If yes, we'll go gently. If not, staying with safety is a complete practice all on its own.
CAP-GATE-b.mp3: One check before we keep going. What's coming works with defensive activation, so let's make sure you actually want to. If there's a real yes — curiosity, not pressure — we'll continue. If it's a no, or a "not today," that's wise; we can stay right here in safety.
DESC-OBJ-TOUCH-a.mp3: What color is it? What shape is it? What temperature is it? What texture is it?
DESC-OBJ-SIGHT-a.mp3: What color is it? What shape is it? What brightness is it? What texture is it?
DESC-OBJ-SOUND-a.mp3: What pitch is it — high, low, somewhere between? Is it a thin or full sound? Layered or simple? What volume is it — loud or soft, or somewhere between? What texture does it have — smooth, rough, sharp, soft?
DESC-OBJ-MOVEMENT-a.mp3: What pace is it — slow, quick, somewhere between? How big is the movement — small or large, subtle or full? What temperature is it — any warmth building? What texture does it have — fluid or stiff, smooth or jerky?
